sourceScope.primarySourceFile: AG 30 - Guideline for the Application of Plan Type to Guaranteed Interest Contracts (GICs) With Benefit Responsive Payment Provisions Used to Fund Emp.pdf
sourceScope.sourceTitle: AG 30 - Guideline for the Application of Plan Type to Guaranteed Interest Contracts (GICs) With Benefit Responsive Payment Provisions Used to Fund Employee Benefit Plans

observedSectionWindows.note: Plan-type background, benefit-responsive GIC payment provisions, C-3 risk reduction, insurer administration, and periodic review boundary.

boundaries: Keep planning focused on this single AG 30 PDF.
Do not widen into AG 31 or later guideline files.
Keep the plan-type / policyholder / C-3-risk context explicit in every batch artifact.
Keep the page image as the backstop for exact wording because the first page contains encoding noise.
Treat the guideline as review-only regulatory guidance, not learner-facing policy text.
Keep the batch source-bound and review-only.
exclusions: No eLearning scripts, slides, quizzes, or app exports.
No raw source files in Git.
No other guideline files in the same wave.

topicMap.label: Plan type and GIC guidance
topicMap.boundaryNote: Keep the two-page window together and do not widen into AG 31.

proposedBatches.title: AG 30 plan type and GIC guidance slice

pageTarget.notes: Capture the full two-page guideline as one review-only slice because the plan-type framing, C-3 risk discussion, and control language belong together.

proposedBatches.rationale: Capture the full two-page guideline as one review-only slice because the plan-type framing, C-3 risk discussion, and control language belong together.

reviewStandards.regulatoryRequirement: Treat the plan-type and withdrawal-treatment language as regulatory text when the guideline directs how benefit-responsive GIC withdrawals should be interpreted.
reviewStandards.definitionOrTerminology: Treat plan type, policyholder, benefit-responsive payment provisions, C-3 risk, and certificate-of-intent references as terminology or context when the text explains rather than directs.
reviewStandards.crossReferenceMapping: Treat references to the Standard Valuation Law, C-3 risk, plan provisions, and insurer administration as cross-reference context unless the text operationalizes them.
reviewStandards.governanceOrControlExpectation: Treat the certificate-of-intent and administration language as governance or control expectation guidance that should remain review-only unless later confirmation makes it operational.
reviewStandards.companyOrPrudentEstimateAssumption: Treat the plan-type assumption and periodic experience review language as assumption-setting context that requires human review before promotion.
reviewStandards.backgroundContent: Treat the title, scene-setting paragraphs, and explanatory transition language as background unless they impose a requirement.
reviewStandards.boundaryControlWindow: Treat the page 1-2 window as a review-only slice and do not split it unless a later cleanup pass explicitly needs that split.
reviewStandards.requiresHumanInterpretation: Treat the encoded first page, plan-type application, and C-3-risk administration examples as requiring human review unless the source text is explicit.

appReady: export is sanitized, stable, and versioned
ragReady: review status no longer requires human disposition

currentChecksNeeded: Plan file exists and parses as JSON.
The AG 30 plan markdown exists and carries the expected headings.
Every proposed batch remains review-only by default.
The source posture is explicitly active.
Each planned batch has a matching batch definition.
The AG 30 batch definition file stays synchronized with the plan.
futureChecksSuggested: No learner-facing, app-ready, or RAG-ready promotion checks.
scriptImplications: scripts/ag30-batch-definitions.mjs should stay synchronized with the planned batch-112 slice when extraction is authorized.
scripts/batch-definitions.mjs should import the AG 30 definition file so the shared runner can find batch-112.
scripts/validate-scaffold.mjs should verify that the AG 30 plan file exists, parses cleanly, and preserves the review-only posture.
The shared runner should continue to honor the same stop conditions used for the earlier guideline batches.

notes: Controlled AG 30 planning artifact. Planned batch 112 remains review-only by default and is not an extraction run.
